gnomad.note: Ashkenazi Jewish founder mutation (5382insC); ClinVar submitter notes report elevated Ashkenazi frequency

insilico.note: PVS1-class frameshift; missense predictors not applicable; CADD approximate

mavedb_function.note: BRCA1 BRCT-domain frameshift; score approximate from saturation-editing region

literature.title: The 5382insC BRCA1 mutation in Ashkenazi Jewish breast and ovarian cancer families.
literature.abstract: Abeliovich et al. reported BRCA1 5382insC, now described as c.5266dup, as a recurrent founder pathogenic variant in Ashkenazi Jewish families with breast and ovarian cancer.

literature.title: Breast and ovarian cancer risks due to inherited mutations in BRCA1 and BRCA2.
literature.abstract: Large carrier-risk analyses include BRCA1 c.5266dup among established pathogenic founder mutations with high breast and ovarian cancer risk.
